items.title: HUD issues PIH Notice implementing FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) funding provisions
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-12 outlines how public housing agencies (PHAs) will receive and apply calendar-year 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding, including $34.557 billion for HAP renewal funding and $2.836 billion for administrative fees. The notice also lists multiple application deadlines, including June 26, 2026 (5 pm local time) for several HAP set-aside categories and July 10, 2026 (5 pm local time) for certain administrative fee requests.

items.title: USDA seeks public comment on SNAP retailer waiver process for healthy-food purchase incentives
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service (FNS) published a notice renewing an information collection for SNAP-authorized retailers that request waivers to offer incentives encouraging healthier purchases. FNS says it receives about 15 incentive waiver requests per year (covering roughly 800 retailers out of about 267,000 authorized SNAP retailers) and set a public comment deadline of July 27, 2026.

items.title: SSA posts 2026–2027 Social Security and SSI payment schedule
items.summary: SSA’s 2026–2027 payment schedule confirms retirement and SSDI benefits are generally paid on the 2nd, 3rd, or 4th Wednesday of each month based on the beneficiary’s birth date (1–10, 11–20, or 21–31). The schedule also lists SSI payments for June 1, 2026 and July 1, 2026.

items.title: Key 2026 Section 8 (HCV) set-aside and special fee deadlines are now posted
items.summary: HUD’s HCV funding notice lists several near-term deadlines for PHAs, including June 26, 2026 (5 pm local time) for multiple HAP set-aside categories and October 30, 2026 (5 pm local time) for certain Special Fees requests. It also states the HAP set-aside totals up to $400 million and Tenant Protection Vouchers (TPV) funding totals $600.622 million for CY 2026.

items.title: IRS reminds taxpayers who missed the April 15 deadline to file as soon as possible and consider payment options
items.summary: In its April 2026 Tax Tip series, the IRS notes the filing and payment deadline for most 2025 federal tax returns was April 15, 2026 and urges people who missed it to submit their return as soon as possible. The IRS also highlights that taxpayers who need help paying can explore available payment options rather than delaying filing.

items.title: CMS releases updated EPSDT coverage guide for children in Medicaid/CHIP
items.summary: A CMS update (summarized by Family Voices) says CMS released updated guidance on May 13, 2026 for Early and Periodic Screening, Diagnostic, and Treatment (EPSDT) coverage in Medicaid and CHIP. The guidance reiterates that states must cover medically necessary services to “correct or ameliorate” conditions for EPSDT-eligible children, including arranging access and informing families about available services.

items.title: IRS highlights its online EITC Assistant for eligibility and credit estimates
items.summary: The IRS directs workers and families to use its EITC Assistant tool to check whether they qualify for the Earned Income Tax Credit and to estimate their credit amount based on filing status and qualifying children. The tool is intended to help households avoid missing out on refundable credits when filing their federal return.
